task_completion.description: Construct a triangle with vertices A(0, 0), B(6, 0), and C(2, 5)
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the GeoGebra Graphics view or Algebra view, there should be three points A, B, and C at approximately (0,0), (6,0), and (2,5), connected by segments to form a triangle.

task_completion.description: Construct the circumcircle of the triangle
task_completion.visual_evidence: A circle passing exactly through all three vertices (A, B, and C) of the triangle should be visible in the Graphics view. The Algebra view may show a 'Circle' command or equation.

task_completion.description: Place a moveable point P constrained to the circumcircle
task_completion.visual_evidence: A point (typically labeled P) should be visible exactly on the edge of the circumcircle. In the Algebra view, it should be defined as a point on the circle object.

task_completion.description: Find the perpendicular projections from P onto the triangle's sides
task_completion.visual_evidence: There should be perpendicular lines dropped from point P to each of the three sides of the triangle (or the extended lines containing the sides). The intersection points (foot points) of these perpendiculars with the sides should be marked.

task_completion.description: Construct the Simson Line through the foot points
task_completion.visual_evidence: A straight line should be drawn passing exactly through the three foot points created in the previous step. These three points must be collinear.

task_completion.description: Include a text annotation mentioning 'Simson Line'
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text box containing the words 'Simson Line' (case-insensitive) should be visible somewhere in the GeoGebra Graphics view.
The GeoGebra window title bar should display 'simson_line' or 'simson_line.ggb', or a save dialog/terminal window should show the file being saved to the specified path.

integrity.description: The agent used the GeoGebra GUI to perform the construction
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the GeoGebra application open with visible tool selections, Algebra view updates, or Graphics view interactions, rather than just terminal commands generating a file.

integrity.description: The Simson Line was constructed using geometric relationships, not drawn arbitrarily
integrity.visual_evidence: The Algebra view should show dependent objects (e.g., PerpendicularLine, Intersect, Line) rather than just independent points manually placed in a line to look correct.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a text editor or image editor to fake the final output
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface shown must be the genuine GeoGebra application with its standard toolbars, sidebars, and rendering style.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected vertices for the triangle are A=(0,0), B=(6,0), and C=(2,5) with a coordinate tolerance of 0.25 units. The circumcircle should have its center around (3, 1.7) with a radius of approximately 3.45. The Simson Line is a valid geometric theorem: the perpendicular projections (foot points) from any point P on the circumcircle onto the three sides of the triangle are always collinear. The final file must be saved exactly as 'simson_line.ggb'.
